content.text:
The adversarial review earned its name — 10 findings, all surviving independent verification, and two of them empirically reproduced by building snapshots on both revisions. The ranked list is reported above; the shape of it matters:

**Don't merge #45 yet.** The five most severe findings are recall regressions in the scanner commit that my bench validation structurally couldn't catch: cross-file namespace merges, generic-typed receivers, regex literals containing `{`, nested destructuring, and loop-head bindings are all rare enough in the nine bench repos that their lost edges drowned in the −6,524 junk-edge win. Aggregate deltas validated the *net* effect; the adversarial pass attacked the *mechanism* and found the corners where it drops real edges or keeps phantom ones. The search commit has two warm-path defects (the preindex cache is derived-but-never-reused, and a transient derivation error hard-fails where the cold path falls back), and the neighbors commit has two CLI-contract bugs.

One consolation: the commit split just paid for itself. The findings map cleanly — five plus the quadratic-scan cleanup onto the scanner commit, two onto search, two onto neighbors, zero onto proto2.

My read on severity: findings 1 and 2 (namespace-merge and generic-receiver edge loss) and 6 (preindex never reused — it silently defeats the entire purpose of `graph index`) are must-fix. Findings 3–5 are real but live in the same scoping helpers and would likely be fixed together, ideally by replacing the per-binding rescans with a single brace-interval pass, which also resolves finding 10. Findings 8–9 are small, contained CLI fixes.

Want me to apply the fixes? I'd amend each fix into its owning commit so the four-commit structure stays clean, re-run the validation battery (bench diff + edge diffs + cache equivalence), and re-run this review to confirm nothing survives.

content.text: Three findings clusters map to disjoint file sets, so I'll fan out three parallel fix agents — scanner (findings 1–5, 10), search warm-path (6–7), neighbors CLI (8–9) — each implementing fixes plus regression tests in the working tree. Then I re-cut the four commits, re-validate, and re-run the adversarial review.

input.description: Fix scanner findings 1-5,10
input.prompt:
You are fixing verified code-review findings in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (Go repo, branch feat/retrieval-followups, working tree clean). Edit ONLY these files: internal/sem/call_scanners.go, internal/sem/provider.go, internal/sem/parser.go (only if a shared helper lives there), internal/sem/typescript_test.go. Do NOT commit, do NOT touch other files, do NOT run git add. Other agents are editing different files concurrently.

Context: this branch adds binding-scope-aware JS/TS call resolution (see the diff of commit 178072d vs its parent: `git show 178072d`). The point of the change is precision: calls to lexically shadowed names must not resolve to same-name namespace/outer symbols. Your fixes must preserve that precision while repairing these six verified regressions:

FINDING 1 (provider.go ~2787 and resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets ~1722): Namespace-classified calls no longer fall back to generic name resolution when no same-file namespace member matches. Repro: src/a.ts `namespace A { export function run() { A.helper(); } }`, src/b.ts `namespace A { export function helper() {} }` — main emitted CALLS run->helper (globally unique name resolution); branch emits nothing. Fix: when resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets finds no same-file target (and the identifier is not shadowed at the call site), fall back to the generic resolveCallTargets path (e.g. add the terminal name to callNames as main did), so cross-file namespace merges resolve again.

FINDING 2 (provider.go ~4270): The TypeScript inherited-method fallback guard changed from `typeName != "" && len(importsByName[typeName]) > 0` (skip only when receiver's annotated type is an imported type) to skipping on ANY non-empty annotation. Repro: `class Base { helper(): void {} } class Worker extends Base { run<T extends Base>(item: T): void { item.helper(); } }` — main emitted CALLS Worker.run -> Base.helper, branch emits nothing because varTypes["item"]="T". Fix: only skip the inheritance-chain fallback when the annotation actually resolves to something (imported type, or a known same-file type that is not in the caller's inheritance chain); a bare generic type parameter or unknown type name must not suppress the fallback.

FINDING 3 (call_scanners.go ~318): jsBraceScopeAt counts braces over stripCodeLiteralsAndComments output, which does NOT mask JS regex literals, so `{` inside a regex literal (e.g. `s.replace(/\{/g, '')`) corrupts the brace stack, breaking jsNamespaceMergesBinding scope equality and jsNamespaceScopes endByte. Fix: mask regex literals for the JS/TS scanning paths (a conservative regex-literal stripper that handles the division-vs-regex ambiguity at least for the common cases: regex after `(`, `,`, `=`, `:`, `return`, start of statement; masking char classes `[...]` and escapes). Apply it wherever the brace counting and namespace/binding scanners consume stripped content.

FINDING 4 (call_scanners.go ~15): jsDestructuredBindingRe `([^{}]*)` and jsArrayBindingRe `([^\[\]]*)` cannot match nested destructuring, so `const { config: { Utils } } = pkg` fails to register Utils as a binding — reintroducing phantom namespace-call edges. Fix: handle nested patterns — e.g. scan the destructuring pattern with a small brace/bracket-balanced extractor instead of a single-level regex, collecting all bound identifiers (aliases after `:` in object patterns, elements in array patterns, rest args, defaults).

FINDING 5 (call_scanners.go ~227): Loop-head bindings (`for (const X of items) {`) get the ENCLOSING brace scope from jsBraceScopeAt (for-parens are not braces), so the shadow covers the whole function/file instead of the loop. Fix: when a lexical binding occurs inside a `for (...)` head, scope it to the loop's following brace block (find the `{` that follows the for-head's closing paren and use that brace pair), falling back to current behavior if no block exists.

FINDING 10 (efficiency, call_scanners.go ~318): jsBraceScopeAt rescans from byte 0 per binding — quadratic on minified bundles. Context: this branch adds binding-scope-aware JS/TS call resolution (see the diff of commit 178072d vs its parent: `git show 178072d`). The point of the change is precision: calls to lexically shadowed names must not resolve to same-name namespace/outer symbols. Your fixes must preserve that precision while repairing these six verified regressions:

FINDING 1 (provider.go ~2787 and resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets ~1722): Namespace-classified calls no longer fall back to generic name resolution when no same-file namespace member matches. Repro: src/a.ts `namespace A { export function run() { A.helper(); } }`, src/b.ts `namespace A { export function helper() {} }` — main emitted CALLS run->helper (globally unique name resolution); branch emits nothing. Fix: when resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets finds no same-file target (and the identifier is not shadowed at the call site), fall back to the generic resolveCallTargets path (e.g. add the terminal name to callNames as main did), so cross-file namespace merges resolve again.

FINDING 2 (provider.go ~4270): The TypeScript inherited-method fallback guard changed from `typeName != "" && len(importsByName[typeName]) > 0` (skip only when receiver's annotated type is an imported type) to skipping on ANY non-empty annotation. Repro: `class Base { helper(): void {} } class Worker extends Base { run<T extends Base>(item: T): void { item.helper(); } }` — main emitted CALLS Worker.run -> Base.helper, branch emits nothing because varTypes["item"]="T". Fix: only skip the inheritance-chain fallback when the annotation actually resolves to something (imported type, or a known same-file type that is not in the caller's inheritance chain); a bare generic type parameter or unknown type name must not suppress the fallback.

FINDING 3 (call_scanners.go ~318): jsBraceScopeAt counts braces over stripCodeLiteralsAndComments output, which does NOT mask JS regex literals, so `{` inside a regex literal (e.g. `s.replace(/\{/g, '')`) corrupts the brace stack, breaking jsNamespaceMergesBinding scope equality and jsNamespaceScopes endByte. Fix: mask regex literals for the JS/TS scanning paths (a conservative regex-literal stripper that handles the division-vs-regex ambiguity at least for the common cases: regex after `(`, `,`, `=`, `:`, `return`, start of statement; masking char classes `[...]` and escapes). Apply it wherever the brace counting and namespace/binding scanners consume stripped content.

FINDING 4 (call_scanners.go ~15): jsDestructuredBindingRe `([^{}]*)` and jsArrayBindingRe `([^\[\]]*)` cannot match nested destructuring, so `const { config: { Utils } } = pkg` fails to register Utils as a binding — reintroducing phantom namespace-call edges. Fix: handle nested patterns — e.g. scan the destructuring pattern with a small brace/bracket-balanced extractor instead of a single-level regex, collecting all bound identifiers (aliases after `:` in object patterns, elements in array patterns, rest args, defaults).

FINDING 5 (call_scanners.go ~227): Loop-head bindings (`for (const X of items) {`) get the ENCLOSING brace scope from jsBraceScopeAt (for-parens are not braces), so the shadow covers the whole function/file instead of the loop. Fix: when a lexical binding occurs inside a `for (...)` head, scope it to the loop's following brace block (find the `{` that follows the for-head's closing paren and use that brace pair), falling back to current behavior if no block exists.

FINDING 10 (efficiency, call_scanners.go ~318): jsBraceScopeAt rescans from byte 0 per binding — quadratic on minified bundles. input.description: Fix search warm-path findings 6-7
input.prompt:
You are fixing verified code-review findings in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (Go repo, branch feat/retrieval-followups, working tree clean). Edit ONLY these files: internal/sem/search.go, internal/sem/search_cache.go, internal/sem/search_cache_test.go, internal/sem/search_test.go. Do NOT commit, do NOT run git add, do NOT touch other files. Other agents are editing different files concurrently.

Context: this branch (commit b6f1e33, see `git show b6f1e33`) reworked search caching: cache keyed by repository identity, relation expansion confined to the query-selected file set. Two verified regressions in the warm path (complete preindex present + selective query), around internal/sem/search.go:349-360:

FINDING 6 (search.go ~354): The warm path unconditionally re-derives the selective snapshot via selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull on EVERY query. It never consults nor persists the per-query selective cache entry that loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot reads/writes on the cold path. Consequence: with a `graph index --profile full` preindex, every repeated identical search re-runs full relation resolution (forEachRelation over selected files + FILE_CHANGES_WITH git scans) at query time — the preindex makes repeated queries SLOWER, defeating its purpose. Fix: make the warm path use the same per-query selective cache discipline as the cold path — check for a valid cached selective snapshot for this query/selection first; only derive from the full snapshot on miss; persist the derived selective snapshot afterwards so the next identical query hits. Reuse existing load/persist helpers rather than duplicating logic.

FINDING 7 (search.go ~357): In the warm path, an error from selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull aborts the whole search, while the equivalent cold path (search_cache.go ~136-146) treats deriveErr as soft and falls back to building a fresh selective snapshot. Repro: preindexed full snapshot loads, then derivation hits a transient failure or provenance mismatch (HEAD advanced between preindex read and derivation) — search exits with 'cached full snapshot provenance mismatch...' where a cache-absent run would succeed. Fix: on derivation error in the warm path, log/ignore consistent with cold-path style and fall back to the fresh build path instead of returning the error.

Requirements:
- Preserve the branch's invariant: a warm cached search must return the same result set as a cold search (there are tests for confinement; keep them passing).
- Add regression tests in search_cache_test.go: (a) warm path persists and reuses the per-query selective entry (e.g. assert the derive/build counter or the cache dir contents show a selective entry after the first warm query and that the second warm query loads it rather than re-deriving — follow existing test patterns for observing cache behavior); (b) warm-path derivation failure falls back to a successful fresh build instead of erroring (simulate a provenance mismatch or inject the failure the way existing tests do).
- Match surrounding style. Verify: gofmt on touched files, `go vet ./internal/sem/`, `go test -count=1 -run 'Search|Cache|Preindex|Selective' ./internal/sem/`, then full `go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/`.
Return: summary of changes per finding, test names added, final test results. Raw facts.

input.description: Fix neighbors CLI findings 8-9
input.prompt:
You are fixing verified code-review findings in /Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph (Go repo, branch feat/retrieval-followups, working tree clean). Edit ONLY these files: internal/cli/neighbors.go, internal/cli/neighbors_efficiency_test.go (or the existing neighbors CLI test file if tests live elsewhere in internal/cli — check first), and if needed internal/cli/search.go ONLY for shared flag-parsing helpers. Do NOT commit, do NOT run git add, do NOT touch other files. Other agents are editing different files concurrently.

Context: this branch (commit 60583c7, `git show 60583c7`) made `neighbors --symbol` accept stable compound-v1 symbol IDs as well as bare names. Two verified CLI bugs:

FINDING 8 (neighbors.go ~297): exactIDFound is set true even when the --file filter rejects the ID-matched symbol; the name/qualified-name fallback is then suppressed, and the command returns an empty result for a real symbol. Repro: pass `--symbol <valid-id>` together with a --file that doesn't string-match the snapshot's repo-relative slash path (stale file arg, `./`-prefixed, absolute, or backslash path — the filter uses EqualFold string comparison). Fix (choose the behavior that makes IDs robust): when --symbol parses as a symbol ID and matches a symbol, the ID's own embedded path should win — normalize the --file comparison (clean the path, strip `./`, convert backslashes, make repo-relative if absolute) and, if the normalized --file still contradicts the ID match, prefer returning the ID-matched definition (documented: the ID already encodes the file) rather than an empty set — or at minimum let the name fallback run instead of suppressing it. Look at how the code reports ambiguity/definitions and keep the UX coherent; an ID lookup that the tool itself printed must never yield a silent empty response.

FINDING 9 (neighbors.go ~226): neighbors still parses --max-context-bytes with searchPositiveIntFlag (rejects 0) while this branch made 0 mean unbounded for `graph search` (internal/cli/search.go uses searchNonNegativeIntFlag now). Fix: use the same non-negative parsing and 0=unbounded semantics in neighbors; ensure the downstream context budgeting treats 0 as unbounded identically to search's implementation.

Requirements:
- Add regression tests following the existing CLI test patterns: (a) ID lookup with a stale/mismatched or non-normalized --file still returns the symbol (or the documented graceful behavior), never empty; (b) `--max-context-bytes 0` accepted by neighbors and behaves as unbounded.
- Match surrounding style. Verify: gofmt on touched files, `go vet ./internal/cli/`, `go test -count=1 ./internal/cli/`.
Return: summary of changes per finding, test names added, final test results. Raw facts.

content.text:
All three fix agents are running in parallel on their disjoint file sets:

1. **Scanner agent** — findings 1–5 and the quadratic scan (10): namespace-merge fallback, receiver-guard narrowing, regex-literal masking, nested destructuring, loop-head scoping, and a single-pass brace index, each with a regression test mirroring the review's repro.
2. **Search agent** — findings 6–7: make the warm path actually reuse and persist the per-query selective cache, and soft-fail derivation errors like the cold path does.
3. **Neighbors agent** — findings 8–9: `--file` normalization/ID-priority so a valid ID lookup can't return silently empty, and `--max-context-bytes 0` parity with search.

Each is required to keep the branch's precision wins intact (the shadowing tests must still pass) and to verify with gofmt, vet, and the package suites before returning. When all three land I'll: verify the combined tree builds and passes the full suite, re-cut the four commits with the fixes folded into their owners, re-run the validation battery (bench comparison, edge diffs, cache equivalence), force-push the branch to update PR #45, and re-run the adversarial review to confirm nothing survives. That's a multi-step tail — I'll report as each milestone completes.

**Finding 9 (`--max-context-bytes 0`)**

Downstream `writeAgentNeighborsBounded` already treats `budget <= 0` as unbounded (writes the full agent output), so 0 behaves identically to search's unbounded semantics; negative values are still rejected.

**Tests (`internal/cli/neighbors_efficiency_test.go`)**
- `TestNeighborsExactSymbolIDDisambiguatesSameFileOverloads` — the mismatched-file block previously asserted the buggy empty result; now asserts the ID-matched definition is returned for stale `other.go`, `./service.go`, `sub\service.go`, and an absolute `/somewhere/else/service.go`.
- `TestNeighborsFileFilterNormalizesPathSpellings` (new) — name lookup with `--file` spelled as `src/one.py`, `./src/one.py`, `src//one.py`, `src\one.py`, and repo-absolute `/repo/root/src/one.py` all select the same single definition.
- `TestNeighborMaxContextBytesZeroMeansUnbounded` (replaces `TestNeighborMaxContextBytesMustBePositive`) — `0` is accepted and parsed as 0, `-1` is rejected with the "non-negative" error, the default remains 16384, and a zero budget produces byte-identical output to the unbounded writer (no `!output-truncated` marker) for a 50-caller response.

**Verification**: `gofmt -l internal/cli/` clean, `go vet ./internal/cli/` clean, `go test -count=1 ./internal/cli/` → ok (2.674s), all three targeted tests PASS. (Build warnings from the third-party `go-tree-sitter/lua` C sources are pre-existing and unrelated.)</result>

<result>Done. My changes are confined to the sem files (the `internal/cli` modifications in git status are from a concurrent agent; `search_test.go` needed no changes).

## Finding 6 — warm path bypassed the per-query selective cache

- `/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/search_cache.go`: added `loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot(ctx, repo, providerVersion, options, cacheDir, disableCache, full)`. It applies the exact cold-path discipline: check the per-query selective cache entry first (`readSearchSnapshot` + `validCachedSearchSnapshot`), on miss derive via `selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull` from the in-memory preindexed snapshot and persist best-effort via `writeSearchSnapshot`/`newCachedSearchSnapshot` (same key computation as `loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot`). No logic duplicated beyond the shared helper calls; guard cases (disabled cache, worktree, empty cache dir, no OnlyFiles, HEAD-resolve failure) delegate to `loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot`.
- `/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph/internal/sem/search.go` (warm branch, ~line 352): replaced the unconditional `selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull` call with `loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot`, updating `cacheHit` from its return so `IndexCacheHit` stays accurate. Repeated identical warm queries now load the persisted selective entry instead of re-running relation resolution + FILE_CHANGES_WITH git scans.

## Finding 7 — warm derivation error aborted the search

- In `loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot`, a `selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull` error is soft (mirrors the cold-path `deriveErr` fall-through at search_cache.go:136-146): it falls back to `loadOrBuildSearchSnapshot`, which builds fresh at the current HEAD. A stale preindex/provenance mismatch no longer surfaces "cached full snapshot provenance mismatch" from `SearchRepository`.

## Tests (search_cache_test.go)

- `TestWarmSelectiveSearchPersistsAndReusesPerQueryCache` (new): first warm query persists the selective entry at the computed key path; second identical query is a cache hit with identical results and unchanged file ModTime (no re-derive/rewrite); after deleting the full preindex entry (re-derivation impossible), the third query still reports `IndexCacheHit` with identical results, proving reuse of the persisted entry.
- `TestWarmSelectiveDerivationFailureFallsBackToFreshBuild` (new): loads the preindexed full snapshot, advances HEAD with an empty commit (simulated provenance mismatch — confirmed `selectiveSearchSnapshotFromFull` errors on it), then asserts the warm loader returns nil error, no cache hit, a snapshot at the advanced HEAD, and `reflect.DeepEqual` with an uncached OnlyFiles build.
- `TestPreindexProviderSnapshotServesSelectiveSearch` (updated): the assertion that warm search must NOT materialize a query-specific cache entry (which enforced the Finding 6 regression) now asserts the entry IS persisted. Its existing warm-vs-uncached result-equality check still passes, preserving the confinement invariant, as does `TestFullPreindexSearchMatchesColdSelectiveGraphExpansion` unchanged.

## Verification

- `gofmt -l` on touched files: clean.
- `go vet ./internal/sem/`: pass (only a pre-existing C warning from the go-tree-sitter/lua dependency).
- `go test -count=1 -run 'Search|Cache|Preindex|Selective' ./internal/sem/`: ok (10.6s).
- Full `go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/`: ok (72.5s).</result>

content.text:
Search warm-path fixes (findings 6–7) are in and verified: a new `loadOrDeriveSelectiveSearchSnapshot` gives the warm path the exact cold-path cache discipline — check the per-query selective entry, derive on miss, persist best-effort — and derivation errors now soft-fall-back to a fresh build instead of aborting. Both regression tests prove the reuse (including serving from the persisted entry after the full preindex is deleted) and the provenance-mismatch fallback. Full `sem` suite green.

One detail worth your attention later: an existing test explicitly asserted the warm path must *not* persist per-query entries — meaning the original WIP made that choice deliberately, and the review classified it as a defect anyway (it makes repeated preindexed queries slower, which defeats the index). The agent flipped that assertion; I agree with the review's call, but it's a design reversal, not just a bug fix, so noting it.

The scanner agent (findings 1–5, 10) is still working — it has the largest surface. Re-cut, validation, and re-review follow once it lands.

<task-notification>
<task-id>a7cc4e36cde1294ac</task-id>
<tool-use-id>REDACTED</tool-use-id>
<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-thomi-Projects-entire-graph/fffff083-8032-496a-81ae-244c3c25ea0f/tasks/a7cc4e36cde1294ac.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Fix scanner findings 1-5,10" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>All six findings fixed. Only my three assigned files were touched (parser.go was not needed); the other modified files in `git status` belong to the concurrent agents.

## Fixes

**Finding 1 — namespace-call fallback** (`internal/sem/provider.go`)
- `resolveJSNamespaceCallTargets` now returns `([]resolvedCallTarget, bool matched)`: `matched=false` means no same-file member of that namespace exists; ambiguous multi-match returns `(nil, true)` so it still refuses to guess.
- Both call sites (per-symbol loop ~2790 and top-level loop ~3160) fall back to generic `resolveCallTargets` on the terminal segment when `!matched` (per-symbol site also guards `terminal != from.Name` and `!childNamesByContainer[from.ID][terminal]`). Shadowed receiver roots never enter `jsNamespaceCalls`, so precision is preserved.

**Finding 2 — inherited-method fallback guard** (`internal/sem/provider.go` ~4280)
- Guard changed from `varTypes[call.Receiver] != ""` to `typeName != "" && typeScriptReceiverTypeResolvesElsewhere(...)`. New helpers `typeScriptReceiverTypeResolvesElsewhere` (imported type, or same-file type-like symbol outside the caller's chain) and `containerChainContains` (cycle/hop-guarded chain walk, mirroring `lookupMethodUpChain`). Bare generic params (`T`) and unknown names no longer suppress the fallback; the two existing skip-tests (imported / local-interface receiver) still pass.

**Finding 3 — regex-literal masking** (`internal/sem/call_scanners.go`)
- Added `stripJSScannedSource` = `maskJSRegexLiterals(stripCodeLiteralsAndComments(...))`, length-preserving. `maskJSRegexLiterals` starts a regex only after `( , = : [ ! & | ? ; { } < > + - * % ~ ^`, start-of-content, or prefix keywords (`return`, `typeof`, `case`, …); body scan handles escapes and `[...]` classes; aborts at newline. Applied in `jsNamespaceScopes`, `jsNamespaceCallIdentifiersAt`, `jsFileBindingScopes`, `jsNamespaceBySymbolID`.

**Finding 4 — nested destructuring** (`internal/sem/call_scanners.go`)
- Replaced the single-level `jsDestructuredBindingRe`/`jsArrayBindingRe` with `jsDestructuringHeadRe` (`\b(const|let|var)\s*([{\[])`) + `findMatchingStaticDelimiter` + `jsAssignmentFollows`. `jsDestructuredNames` is now recursive: strips top-level defaults (`=`), object aliases via top-level `:` (new `jsTopLevelByteIndex` depth-aware scanner), rest `...`, and recurses into nested `{}`/`[]` patterns. Also improves nested destructured *parameters* via `jsParameterNames`.

**Finding 5 — loop-head binding scope** (`internal/sem/call_scanners.go`)
- New `jsForHeadBindingScope`: for `const`/`let` declared inside a `for (...)`/`for await (...)` head, the binding scope becomes the loop's following brace block; falls back to the enclosing brace scope when no block exists. `var` keeps its function-scope handling (correct JS semantics).

**Finding 10 — quadratic brace scan** (`internal/sem/call_scanners.go`)
- Removed `jsBraceScopeAt` (O(n) per binding). New `jsBraceScopes` struct built in one pass (`opens`/`closes`/`parents`), with `enclosing(position)` doing binary search + parent walk — O(n) build, O(log n + depth) per lookup, same semantics including unmatched-brace cases. Threaded through `jsFileBindingScopes` and `jsNamespaceMergesBinding`.

## Tests added (internal/sem/typescript_test.go)

- `TestTypeScriptCrossFileNamespaceMergeCallResolves` (finding 1)
- `TestTypeScriptGenericReceiverKeepsInheritedMethodFallback` (finding 2)
- `TestTypeScriptRegexLiteralBraceDoesNotCorruptBindingScopes` (finding 3)
- `TestTypeScriptNestedDestructuringShadowsNamespaceCalls` (finding 4; object + array nesting, plus unshadowed control)
- `TestTypeScriptLoopHeadBindingShadowConfinedToLoop` (finding 5; post-loop call resolves, in-loop call stays shadowed)

All five verified to FAIL against the pre-fix HEAD (via a throwaway worktree, since removed) and pass with the fixes.

## Verification

- `gofmt -s -w` applied; `gofmt -l internal/sem` reports nothing for the touched files
- `go vet ./internal/sem/` clean (only cgo warnings from the vendored tree-sitter dependency)
- `go test -count=1 -run 'TypeScript|Javascript|JS|Namespace|Binding|Scope' ./internal/sem/` → ok
- Full `go test -count=1 ./internal/sem/` → ok (30.8s), all pre-existing precision tests (shadowed-name suppression, minified-bundle false-positive guards) still pass</result>
<usage><subagent_tokens>140671</subagent_tokens><tool_uses>50</tool_uses><duration_ms>837654</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

S=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-thomi-Projects-entire-graph/fffff083-8032-496a-81ae-244c3c25ea0f/scratchpad
git reset -q --soft main && git reset -q
git add internal/sem/protobuf.go internal/sem/protobuf_test.go
git commit -q -C eaf9c58
git diff README.md | awk '/^@@ -190,/{skip=1; next} /^@@/{skip=0} !skip || /^diff|^index|^---|^\+\+\+/' > $S/readme_n2.patch
git apply --cached $S/readme_n2.patch
git add internal/cli/neighbors.go internal/cli/neighbors_efficiency_test.go internal/cli/root.go
git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(cli): accept stable symbol IDs in neighbors --symbol

--symbol now resolves compound-v1 symbol IDs as well as bare names, so
agents can follow up a search hit without re-disambiguating by name and
--file. An ID match ignores the --file filter (the ID encodes its file),
name-fallback file filters normalize path spellings, and
--max-context-bytes 0 means unbounded, matching graph search.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01AS8YV3giRoWagbRFo3ihAX
EOF
)"
git add internal/sem/call_scanners.go internal/sem/parser.go internal/sem/model.go internal/sem/provider.go internal/sem/typescript_test.go
git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(sem): binding-scope-aware JS/TS call resolution

Track lexical binding scopes (parameters, locals, nested destructuring,
loop heads, namespace merges) when resolving direct and namespace-
qualified JS/TS calls, so a call to a shadowed name no longer resolves
to an arbitrary same-name symbol in an outer scope, while unmatched
namespace calls still fall back to workspace-wide resolution and
receiver-type guards only suppress inheritance fallbacks for types that
resolve elsewhere. Regex literals are masked before brace scanning, and
brace scopes are indexed in a single pass per file. Also stop
attributing calls to symbols extracted from inline type-literal
properties. Eliminates large false-positive edge families in minified
bundles (lodash dist: -6.5k bogus "exact" CALLS edges) while adding
real cross-package resolutions (trpc: +337) in benchmark repos.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01AS8YV3giRoWagbRFo3ihAX
EOF
)"
git add -A
git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(search): cache confinement, stable tie-breaking, unbounded context

Key cached search snapshots by repository identity and confine relation
expansion served from a complete prepared cache to the query-selected
file set, so a warm cache returns the same result set as a cold search.
The warm path reuses and persists per-query selective snapshots with
the same discipline as the cold path and falls back to a fresh build on
derivation errors. Break location-tied candidate ordering by semantic
identity for stable output, and allow --max-context-bytes 0 to mean
unbounded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01AS8YV3giRoWagbRFo3ihAX
EOF
)"
